NOTICE OF X-COMPLAINANTS' FAILURE TO OPPOSE X-DEFT'S DEMURRER FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS-DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION WERE NOT OPPOSED. FROM THE FACE OF THE CROSS COMPLAINT, THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT CLEARLY AND AFFIRMATIVELY TIME-BARRED. THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, HOWEVER, DO NOT ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR EQUITABLE INDEMNITY. CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS GIVEN TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR TOTAL AND COMPARATIVE EQUITABLE INDEMNITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JAMES MCBRIDE; COURT REPORTER: TEANNA L. WARD, CSR NO. 119118

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS-DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS NOT OPPOSED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JAMES MCBRIDE; COURT REPORTER: TEANNA L. WARD, CSR NO. 119118

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2012 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with fifteen days leave to amend. The second and third causes of action do not allege sufficient ultimate facts to constitute cognizable claims for equitable indemnity. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-07-2012 Continued To Nov-27-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-23-2012 Continued To Sep-25-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-12-2012 Continued To Aug-07-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BENJAMIN C.O. ANDERSON, TEL. NO. (925) 930-9550 APPEARING FOR THE
CROSS-DEFENDANT ;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend as to the first and sixth causes of action. Sustained with leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action. The demurrer to the first and sixth causes of action were not opposed. From the face of the cross complaint, the second and third causes of action are not clearly and affirmatively time-barred. The second and third causes of action, however, do not allege facts sufficient to constitute causes of action for equitable indemnity. Cross-Complainant is given ten days leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege facts sufficient to constitute causes of action for total and comparative equitable indemnity. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: James Mcbride; Court Reporter: Teanna L. Ward, CSR No. 119118 = (302/JJM)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
